title: B. Sc. (Hons. With Research) Economics
content: Applications of economic theories are now widespread in the government, industry, international and national organizations, finance, money and banking, and also in the all-pervading field of data driven decision making. Keeping the current trends in economic studies in mind, the B Sc (Honours) course in Economics at the Galgotias University introduces the state-of-the-art tools such as Python and R programming and Artificial Intelligence (AI) and their applications in the economic science. The multi-disciplinary approach of the course including economics, computer science and research methods immensely benefit the students. *4-year Undergraduate Degree (Honours with Research) This is to inform all the concerned that as envisaged in NEP2020, the University has introduced a 4-year Undergraduate (UG) Degree (Honours with Research) from the academic year 2022-23 The students who secure 75% marks or a 7.5 CGPAand above, in the first six semesters, and wish to undertake research at the UG level can choose a research stream in the fourth year. Such students should do a research project or dissertation under the guidance of a faculty member of the University. The research project/dissertation will be in the major discipline. The students who secure 160 credits, including 12 credits from a research project/dissertation, are awarded a 4-year UG Degree (Honours with Research). S. No. | Broad Category of Course | Minimum Credit Requirement for a4-Year UG Programme | 1 | Major (Core) | 80 | 2 | Minor Stream | 32 | 3 | Multidisciplinary | 09 | 4 | Ability Enhancement Courses (AEC) | 08 | 5 | Skill Enhancement Courses (SEC) | 09 | 6 | Value Added Courses common for all UG | 06-08 | 7 | Summer Internship | 02-04 | 8 | Research Project/ Dissertation | 12 | 9 | Total | 160 | 4-year UG Degree with Honours # In the event, students fail to secure 75% marks or a 7.5 CGPA in the first six semesters, they will be awarded a 4-year UG Degree with Honours only. In addition, students not undertaking research will do 3 courses for 12 credits in lieu of a research project / Dissertation. (Source: - UGC Curriculum & Credit Framework for Undergraduate Programmes) XII with Maths with minimum 50% marks in aggregate Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list Graduates of this program can explore diverse career opportunities, such as: 01 Strong foundation in economic theory, applied mathematics, and statistical analysis 02 Emphasis on quantitative tools, including econometrics, R, Python, and AI applications 03 Interdisciplinary curriculum integrating environmental, health, industrial, and sustainability economics 04 Practical exposure through field research, internships, and lab-based statistical training 05 Dedicated final year for research dissertation, project work, and academic publications 06 Skill enhancement through value-added and entrepreneurship-oriented courses 07 Ideal for students aiming for careers in data science, policy think tanks, global institutions, and analytics 01 Understand and apply the fundamental economic principles, concepts, and methods in key areas of economic science and related fields. 02 Take in-depth understanding of the statistical software used in econometrics and will be able to apply these tools to solve problems in economics; 03 Demonstrate problem solving, analytical and logical skills to provide solutions for the scientific research requirements 04 Attain programming skills like python, R, and, knowledge and applications of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the field of economics 05 Understand the importance of subject knowledge for the growth and encouragement of scientific research. 06 Gain knowledge from the multidisciplinary aspects of modern economic discourse. 07 Enhance their capability, value added awareness and knowledge to compete for ‘vocal to local’. 08 Engage in continuous thoughtful learning in environmental aspects, and, life-long learning and professional communication among the students. 09 Engage in field research and internship activity to explore more real-world problems solving scenarios. 10 Use analytical prowess in different interdisciplinary fields such as environmental, health, AI, manufacturability, sustainability and so on. 01 Contribute to the functioning and growth of industries, businesses, and, the government and international organisations. 02 Set up their own start-up firm and operate business individually or in teams. 03 Graduates will pursue higher studies and provide reliable research outcomes 01 Understand the basic knowledge of different branches of economics and its quantitative analysis. 02 Acquire analytical knowledge, tools and techniques using statistical software. 03 Acquisition of in-depth knowledge of multidisciplinary, skill enhancement and value-added courses
